supply_chain.note: The rosetta corpus is a DOCS repo — no package.json / go.mod / requirements.txt / lockfile anywhere in it, so there is nothing to audit corpus-side. All tooling code + deps live in the separate rosetta-extensions repo (authoring copy .agentspace/rosetta-extensions @ 498b1a5), audited per-milestone; v2.6 added no new third-party dependency (docs + tooling-test + render/bash-only release). npm_vulnerabilities not re-run at release close (0 new deps; per-milestone audits stand).

measurement_method.go_reproducible_funcs: git grep -E '^func Test' -- '*.go' over the whole rext repo — the cross-release like-for-like anchor re-anchored at v2.5 M234. 2010 at HEAD 498b1a5.
measurement_method.go_func_snapshot_note: per-milestone metrics recorded 2005 at M241-M243; the +5 to 2010 came from M244 iters/harden (contentsession iter-06 + denominator fences). 2010 is the authoritative final-tag reading.
measurement_method.typescript: playwright test on the *.unit.spec.ts subset, tallied from the runner's own pass line; live-browser specs enumerated but NOT executed at close (need a running demo).
measurement_method.python: per-milestone JUnit/pytest tallies; the demo-stack section is documented HOST-SENSITIVE (pass/skip counts vary with ambient stack state) so its full-suite number drifts across milestones as tests were added.
measurement_method.coverage: no line-coverage tool is wired in the rext repo (behaviour-path + mutation-verify convention, same as v2.5's non-Go sections); the one numeric anchor is the Go stack-seeding module. Touched packages re-measured directly at close (go test -cover).

regression_gate.note: All three hard regression conditions pass. Two YELLOW annotations recorded (not gate-blockers, do not trip any of the three conditions): (1) the 8 standing demo-stack failures are documented chronic test-side debt (0 real defects) routed to the BLOCKING release-close deferral audit; (2) a net-new enforcement-wiring finding at the final tag — run-unit.sh's roster + its UnitSpecsAreExecuted guard are RED because 2 harden-added unit specs were not rostered (the tests themselves pass). See close_findings.

stack_verify_roster_drift.severity: YELLOW (real, 0 product impact, trivially fixable — NOT a gate condition)
stack_verify_roster_drift.what: At the final consumption tag 498b1a5, run-unit.sh's explicit UNIT_SPECS roster lists 7 of the 9 *.unit.spec.ts files on disk. The 2 unrostered specs (content-denominator.unit.spec.ts, run-discrete.unit.spec.ts) were ADDED by the M244 final harden (passes 2 & 5) but never added to the roster.
stack_verify_roster_drift.consequence: run-unit.sh refuses (exit 2) and its own integrity guard test_e2e_collection_integrity.py::UnitSpecsAreExecuted is RED (2 sub-tests: the on-disk-but-unrostered name-check + the execute-for-real). This is the exact 'a pin nobody runs is not a pin' defect that guard was built to catch, reintroduced by the harden that added the specs.
stack_verify_roster_drift.impact: The recorded 172 green count is ACCURATE — all 9 specs pass when invoked directly (172; the 7 rostered alone = 162). No product/tooling behaviour is wrong; the tests pass. Only the canonical rostered runner + guard are out of sync.
stack_verify_roster_drift.fix: Add the 2 spec filenames to run-unit.sh's UNIT_SPECS array (2-line change). Orchestrator Phase 7.
